text: The dynamic MRI scan shows sagittal views of the pelvic floor reconstruction with biological mesh in a male patient post-extra levator abdominoperineal excision of rectal cancer. A red arrow highlights the reconstructed pelvic floor, which appears stable without herniation or abnormal descent at rest and during the Valsalva maneuver.

Sagittal views of dynamic MRI scans show the reconstructed pelvic floor of a male patient who underwent perineal reconstruction. A red pointer highlights the pelvic floor, which exhibits no herniation or abnormal descent at rest or during a Valsalva maneuver.

The MRI footage displays a perineal hernia through a pelvic floor defect following an extra levator abdominoperineal excision of rectal cancer. At rest, the hernia is visible, and during the Valsalva maneuver, further herniation of the small bowel into the perineal defect is observed.
